body.title: CLOSED: proposals queue: occurrence_count + first_seen_at — recurrence signal for Igor
body.result: shipped alongside T-igor-proposals-queue — occurrence_count + first_seen_at already in schema (42a44a45)
body.description:
Extend instance.proposals with: occurrence_count int DEFAULT 1, first_seen_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(). When add_proposal() is called for a proposal matching an existing pending one (same kind + md5(content[:200])), increment occurrence_count rather than inserting a new row. Igor reads occurrence_count when deciding whether to commit — the count is informational, never an auto-gate. This is the calligraphy principle: recurrence + foreseen future use drives Igor's own promotion judgment, not an automatic counter.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/cognition/proposals.py` (dedup logic in add_proposal()), DB migration (occurrence_count + first_seen_at on instance.proposals)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; direct psycopg2; dedup on md5(kind + content[:200]) — deterministic, not semantic; occurrence_count is observable by Igor but never auto-commits
**Scope boundary:** IN — two columns, dedup in add_proposal(), count returned in read_pending(). OUT — no auto-commit logic, no semantic dedup.
**Test plan:** two add_proposal() calls with identical kind+content[:200] → single row with occurrence_count=2; different content → two rows; read_pending() includes occurrence_count
